CheckTitle: AI Agent Dependencies Must Use Exact Pinned Versions with Lock Files
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Supply Chain Security**. Validates that all agent dependencies are pinned to exact versions using lock files (poetry.lock, requirements.txt with hashes, package-lock.json). Floating versions allow dependency confusion and typosquatting attacks.
Risk: Unpinned or range-based dependency versions can resolve to different package versions across builds. Attackers can exploit this through dependency confusion, typosquatting, or by publishing a malicious patch version that satisfies a floating constraint.

Code.Other:
1. Use a lock file (poetry.lock, package-lock.json, Pipfile.lock) and commit it to version control.
2. Pin all direct and transitive dependencies to exact versions.
3. Enable hash-checking mode in pip (--require-hashes) or equivalent.
4. Audit lock files on every merge with tools such as pip-audit or npm audit.

Recommendation.Text: Pin all dependencies to exact versions and commit lock files to version control. Enable hash verification during installation to prevent dependency substitution attacks.

Notes: ASPM-071 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
